name: Rep. Cresent Hardy
tweet: Nevadans don’t want more regulations, they want more #jobs.

name: Rep. Cresent Hardy
tweet: Instead of ending too-big to fail, regulators inserted it into law.

name: Rep. Cresent Hardy
tweet: Dodd-Frank in essence is top-down governance from Washington Bureaucrats.

name: Rep. Cresent Hardy
tweet: These community banks were not the banks making risky loans, they were building strong relationships with their customers.

name: Rep. Cresent Hardy
tweet: These small community banks serve my constituents. They serve the neighborhoods in my district.

name: Rep. Cresent Hardy
tweet: One size-fits all rules have impacted small bankers so much that we have less community banks now than we had before Dodd-Frank.

name: Rep. Cresent Hardy
tweet: They are being suffocated by the 400 new federal regulations.

name: Rep. Cresent Hardy
tweet: Not surprisingly, Dodd-Frank took a big swing at our community banks as well.

name: Rep. Cresent Hardy
tweet: Half of all people employed in this country work for small businesses.

name: Rep. Cresent Hardy
tweet: The life blood of our nation lies with small businesses.

name: Rep. Cresent Hardy
tweet: Think about that – we have more small business “deaths” than small business “births.”

name: Rep. Cresent Hardy
tweet: We have, for the first time in over three decades, more small businesses failing than being started.

name: Rep. Cresent Hardy
tweet: Five years in, what do we have today?

name: Rep. Cresent Hardy
tweet: This week marks the fifth anniversary of the signing of a law that was the Democratic answer to the recession that impacted our nation.
